topics.name: Number System
topics.notes: Classification: Natural, Whole, Integers, Rational, Irrational, Real numbers. Divisibility rules for 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 9, 11. Even/odd and prime/composite numbers. Face value vs place value. Sum of first n natural numbers = n(n+1)/2; sum of squares = n(n+1)(2n+1)/6. Divisibility tests are asked directly in almost every RRB paper.

topics.name: HCF and LCM
topics.notes: HCF (GCD) = highest common factor; LCM = lowest common multiple. Product of two numbers = HCF Ã— LCM. For fractions: HCF = HCF(numerators)/LCM(denominators); LCM = LCM(numerators)/HCF(denominators). Use prime factorisation or long division method. Common application: bells-ringing-together / minimum-tiles type problems.

topics.name: Simplification & Approximation
topics.notes: Follow BODMAS/VBODMAS strictly. Memorise squares (1-30), cubes (1-15) and tables (1-25) for speed. Approximation questions ask for the 'nearest value' â€” round each term before calculating instead of computing the exact answer.

topics.name: Decimals and Fractions
topics.notes: Convert decimals to fractions using place value (0.25 = 25/100 = 1/4). Pure recurring decimal 0.(ab) = ab/99; mixed recurring = (whole minus non-recurring part) / (9s and 0s). Compare fractions by cross-multiplication.

topics.name: Ratio and Proportion
topics.notes: a:b = a/b. If a:b = c:d then ad = bc (cross-product rule). Types: direct, inverse, compound proportion. Mixture & alligation problems combine ratio with averages and are common in RRB papers.

topics.name: Percentage
topics.notes: Percent means 'per hundred'. To increase/decrease a number by x%, multiply by (100Â±x)/100. Successive percentage change: net% = a + b + (ab/100). Memorise common fraction-to-% conversions (1/8=12.5%, 1/3=33.33%, 1/6=16.67%) for speed.

topics.name: Profit, Loss and Discount
topics.notes: Profit = SP âˆ’ CP; Loss = CP âˆ’ SP. Profit% = (Profit/CP) Ã— 100. SP after discount = MP Ã— (100 âˆ’ discount%)/100. Successive discounts are combined the same way as successive percentage changes.

topics.name: Simple & Compound Interest
topics.notes: SI = (P Ã— R Ã— T)/100. CI = P[(1+R/100)^T âˆ’ 1]. CI > SI whenever T > 1. Half-yearly compounding: halve the rate, double the time. Difference between CI and SI over 2 years = P(R/100)Â².

topics.name: Time and Work
topics.notes: If A finishes work in 'a' days, A's one-day work = 1/a. For combined work, add individual one-day work rates. Wages should be split in the ratio of work actually done (efficiency Ã— days worked).

topics.name: Time, Speed and Distance
topics.notes: Speed = Distance/Time. km/h â†’ m/s: multiply by 5/18; m/s â†’ km/h: multiply by 18/5. Relative speed: add if moving opposite directions, subtract if same direction. Boats & streams: downstream = u+v, upstream = uâˆ’v.

topics.name: Mensuration â€” 2D
topics.notes: Rectangle area = lÃ—b. Square area = sÂ². Triangle area = Â½Ã—baseÃ—height, or Heron's formula âˆš[s(sâˆ’a)(sâˆ’b)(sâˆ’c)]. Circle area = Ï€rÂ², circumference = 2Ï€r. Trapezium area = Â½Ã—(sum of parallel sides)Ã—height.

topics.name: Mensuration â€” 3D
topics.notes: Cuboid: volume=lÃ—bÃ—h, surface area=2(lb+bh+hl). Cube: volume=sÂ³. Cylinder: volume=Ï€rÂ²h, curved surface area=2Ï€rh. Cone: volume=â…“Ï€rÂ²h, slant height=âˆš(rÂ²+hÂ²). Sphere: volume=4/3Ï€rÂ³, surface area=4Ï€rÂ².

topics.name: Algebra
topics.notes: Key identities: (a+b)Â²=aÂ²+2ab+bÂ²; (aâˆ’b)Â²=aÂ²âˆ’2ab+bÂ²; aÂ²âˆ’bÂ²=(a+b)(aâˆ’b); (a+b)Â³=aÂ³+bÂ³+3ab(a+b). Quadratic equation axÂ²+bx+c=0 has roots x=[âˆ’bÂ±âˆš(bÂ²âˆ’4ac)]/2a.

topics.name: Geometry
topics.notes: Triangle's angles sum to 180Â°. Pythagoras theorem (right triangles only): hypotenuseÂ²=baseÂ²+heightÂ². Angle in a semicircle = 90Â°. Quadrilateral angles sum to 360Â°. Congruence/similarity rules: SSS, SAS, ASA, AA.

topics.name: Trigonometry
topics.notes: sinÎ¸=opposite/hypotenuse, cosÎ¸=adjacent/hypotenuse, tanÎ¸=opposite/adjacent. Memorise standard values for 0Â°, 30Â°, 45Â°, 60Â°, 90Â°. Identity: sinÂ²Î¸+cosÂ²Î¸=1. Height & distance problems use angle of elevation/depression with tanÎ¸.

topics.name: Elementary Statistics
topics.notes: Mean = sum of observations / count. Median = middle value when arranged in order (average of two middle values if count is even). Mode = most frequent value. Practice reading bar graphs, pie charts and tables quickly.

topics.name: Square Roots & Cube Roots
topics.notes: Find square roots by prime factorisation or the division method. Cube roots: factorise into groups of 3 identical primes. Memorise squares up to 30Â² and cubes up to 15Â² for exam speed.

topics.name: Problems on Ages
topics.notes: Set up an equation using 'present age' as the variable, then apply the time condition given (past/future). If a ratio of ages is given, assume the ages as multiples of a common variable (e.g., 3x and 5x).

topics.name: Calendar and Clock
topics.notes: A normal year has 1 odd day (365 days = 52 weeks + 1); a leap year has 2. Use 'odd days' to find the day of the week for any date. Clock: each hour-mark=30Â°, minute hand moves 6Â°/min, hour hand moves 0.5Â°/min; angle between hands = |30H âˆ’ 11M/2|.

topics.name: Pipes and Cisterns
topics.notes: Same logic as Time & Work â€” a pipe filling a tank in 'x' hours fills 1/x of it per hour. Outlet (emptying) pipes count as negative work. Net rate per hour = sum of inlet rates âˆ’ sum of outlet rates.

topics.name: Data Interpretation
topics.notes: Read the chart/table and the exact question carefully before calculating. Compute only what's asked rather than every value in the table. Practise quick approximation to save time under exam pressure.

topics.name: Averages
topics.notes: Average = sum of items / number of items. If a value is added/removed from a group: new average = (old sum Â± value)/new count. Average speed for equal distances at two speeds = 2xy/(x+y).

topics.name: Analogy
topics.notes: Find the relationship between a given pair of words/numbers/letters and apply the same logic to find the missing term (letter shift, opposite, part-whole, etc.). Practice both word and number analogies.

topics.name: Series Completion
topics.notes: Identify the pattern: arithmetic (constant difference), geometric (constant ratio), alternating, or a mix. For alphabet series, convert letters to numbers (A=1...Z=26) to spot the pattern faster.

topics.name: Coding-Decoding
topics.notes: Common types: letter shifting (+1/âˆ’1 etc.), substitution coding, number coding. Decode the given example pattern first, then apply the exact same rule to the word/code asked in the question.

topics.name: Mathematical Operations
topics.notes: Symbols are swapped with operators (e.g., '+' actually means 'Ã—'). Substitute carefully, then solve using BODMAS with the new meanings â€” don't let the swapped symbols confuse you.

topics.name: Similarities & Differences
topics.notes: Classification ('odd one out') questions test which item doesn't share the common property of the rest of the group â€” check the category (e.g., all are mammals except one) or the numeric pattern (all are primes except one).

topics.name: Blood Relations
topics.notes: Draw a quick family-tree diagram as you read the statements â€” this avoids confusion across generations and in-laws. Know terms like paternal/maternal and in-law relationships.

topics.name: Analytical Reasoning
topics.notes: Read all the given statements fully before drawing any conclusion. Use elimination across the answer options when the relationships described are complex or layered.

topics.name: Syllogism
topics.notes: Use Venn diagrams to test 'All', 'Some' and 'No' statements. Remember: 'Some A are B' doesn't make 'Some A are not B' automatically false â€” both can be true depending on context.

topics.name: Sentence/Word Arrangement
topics.notes: For jumbled sentences, identify the logical connector or chronological order. For seating arrangement, draw a diagram (linear/circular), place fixed clues first, then work out relative positions.

topics.name: Venn Diagrams
topics.notes: Used to represent set relationships and solve overlapping-category problems (e.g., students who play cricket and/or football). Practise both 2-circle and 3-circle Venn diagrams.

topics.name: Puzzle & Seating Arrangement
topics.notes: Draw the seating diagram carefully and note the 'facing direction' if mentioned â€” it changes which side is left/right. Solve fixed-position clues before relative-position clues.

topics.name: Data Sufficiency
topics.notes: Decide whether the given statement(s) â€” alone, together, or neither â€” are sufficient to answer the question. You don't need to actually find the answer, only judge sufficiency.

topics.name: Statement & Conclusion
topics.notes: Check if a conclusion logically and necessarily follows from the statement(s) alone â€” don't bring in outside/general knowledge while judging this.

topics.name: Direction Sense
topics.notes: Draw the path on paper using N-E-S-W. Standard rule: facing north, a right turn faces east, a left turn faces west. Net displacement problems usually use the Pythagoras theorem when the path forms a right angle.

topics.name: Non-verbal Reasoning
topics.notes: Mirror image flips left-right (vertical mirror); water image flips top-bottom and is upside down (horizontal mirror). Practise figure series and figure-matching (counting sides/shapes) for speed.

topics.name: Motion & Force
topics.notes: Newton's three laws of motion. SI unit of force = Newton. Speed = distance/time; velocity is speed with direction. Acceleration = change in velocity / time.

topics.name: Work, Energy & Power
topics.notes: Work = Force Ã— Displacement (Joule). Kinetic energy = Â½mvÂ²; Potential energy = mgh. Power = Work/Time (Watt). Law of conservation of energy: energy can neither be created nor destroyed.

topics.name: Light
topics.notes: Reflection: angle of incidence = angle of reflection. Refraction occurs when light passes between media of different density. Convex lens converges light; concave lens diverges it (mirror conventions are the opposite of lenses â€” be careful).

topics.name: Electricity & Magnetism
topics.notes: Current (Ampere) = charge/time. Ohm's Law: V = IR. Series circuit: same current, voltage divides. Parallel circuit: same voltage, current divides. Right-hand thumb rule gives the magnetic field direction around a current-carrying wire.

topics.name: Heat
topics.notes: Heat flows from hot to cold via conduction, convection or radiation. SI unit of temperature = Kelvin. Specific heat capacity = heat needed to raise 1 kg of a substance by 1Â°C.

topics.name: Sound
topics.notes: Sound needs a medium to travel â€” it cannot travel in a vacuum. Speed of sound in air â‰ˆ 343 m/s; it travels faster in solids than liquids than gases. Frequency determines pitch; amplitude determines loudness.

topics.name: Matter & States
topics.notes: Matter exists as solid, liquid, gas, and plasma. Changes of state: melting, boiling, freezing, condensation, sublimation. Mixtures can be homogeneous or heterogeneous.

topics.name: Atomic Structure
topics.notes: An atom has a nucleus (protons + neutrons) with electrons in shells around it. Atomic number = number of protons; mass number = protons + neutrons. Isotopes share the atomic number but differ in mass number.

topics.name: Acids, Bases & Salts
topics.notes: Acids turn blue litmus red (pH<7); bases turn red litmus blue (pH>7); pH 7 is neutral. Common acids: HCl (stomach), citric acid (citrus fruit), acetic acid (vinegar). Common bases: NaOH, Ca(OH)â‚‚.

topics.name: Periodic Table
topics.notes: Elements are arranged by increasing atomic number. Groups (vertical columns) share similar properties; periods (horizontal rows) show a gradual property trend. Noble gases (Group 18) are chemically inert.

topics.name: Carbon Compounds
topics.notes: Carbon forms covalent bonds and long chains (catenation). Hydrocarbons: alkanes (single bonds), alkenes (one double bond), alkynes (one triple bond). Organic compounds form the basis of life and most fuels.

topics.name: Chemical Reactions
topics.notes: Types: combination, decomposition, displacement, double displacement, oxidation-reduction. When balancing equations, the number of atoms of each element must match on both sides.

topics.name: Cell Structure
topics.notes: The cell is the basic unit of life. Plant cells have a cell wall, chloroplast and large vacuole; animal cells don't. The nucleus controls cell activities and holds DNA. Mitochondria is the cell's 'powerhouse'.

topics.name: Human Body Systems
topics.notes: Major systems: digestive, respiratory, circulatory (4-chambered heart), nervous (brain & spinal cord), skeletal (206 bones in adults), endocrine (hormones). Know the basic function of each major organ.

topics.name: Diseases & Immunity
topics.notes: Diseases can be communicable (caused by pathogens like bacteria/viruses) or non-communicable (lifestyle/genetic). Vaccines build immunity by exposing the body to a weakened or inactive form of a pathogen.

topics.name: Nutrition & Vitamins
topics.notes: Carbohydrates & fats give energy; proteins aid growth and repair. Deficiency diseases: Vitamin A (night blindness), Vitamin C (scurvy), Vitamin D (rickets), Vitamin B1 (beriberi). Minerals: iron (anaemia), iodine (goitre), calcium (bones).

topics.name: Current Affairs
topics.notes: Use the Current Affairs section of this app for the latest news â€” current affairs from the last 6-12 months carry the highest weightage in the GA section of RRB exams. Review it regularly, not just before the exam.

topics.name: History â€” Ancient & Medieval
topics.notes: Indus Valley Civilization, Vedic period, Mauryan & Gupta empires, major medieval dynasties (Delhi Sultanate, Mughals, Vijayanagara). Focus on important rulers, their achievements, and major monuments.

topics.name: History â€” Modern & Freedom Struggle
topics.notes: 1857 Revolt, formation of the INC (1885), partition of Bengal, Gandhi's movements (Non-Cooperation, Civil Disobedience, Quit India), key freedom fighters and their roles, Independence & Partition (1947).

topics.name: Geography â€” Physical (India)
topics.notes: Major mountain ranges (Himalayas, Western/Eastern Ghats), major rivers (Ganga, Brahmaputra, Godavari etc. â€” source, length, tributaries), India's climate types and soil types.

topics.name: Geography â€” Economic Resources
topics.notes: Major crops by region, mineral resources (coal & iron-ore producing states), key industrial regions, India's neighbouring countries and the states bordering them.

topics.name: World Geography Basics
topics.notes: Continents and oceans, major international boundaries, the world's longest rivers / highest mountains / largest deserts, and capital cities of major countries.

topics.name: Indian Polity & Constitution
topics.notes: Constitution adopted 26 Nov 1949, effective from 26 Jan 1950. Fundamental Rights (Art. 12-35), Directive Principles, structure of Parliament (Lok Sabha & Rajya Sabha), powers of the President & PM, and key constitutional amendments.

topics.name: Indian Economy Basics
topics.notes: Key terms: GDP, GNP, inflation, fiscal deficit, repo rate. Five-Year Plans and NITI Aayog. Structure of banking (RBI as central bank; public/private/cooperative banks). Major economic schemes.

topics.name: Environment & Ecology
topics.notes: India's biodiversity hotspots, major national parks and tiger reserves, climate change basics (greenhouse effect, global warming), and key international agreements (Paris Agreement, Kyoto Protocol).

topics.name: Inventions & Discoveries
topics.notes: Key inventors and their inventions (telephone â€” Bell, light bulb â€” Edison, etc.), Indian scientists and their work (Raman, Bose, Bhabha, Sarabhai, Kalam), and Nobel laureates from India.

topics.name: Sports GK
topics.notes: Major events (Olympics, Commonwealth Games, Asian Games) with their venues/years, important trophies & cups by sport, sports terminology, and national sports awards (Khel Ratna, Arjuna Award).

topics.name: Indian Art & Culture
topics.notes: Classical dance forms (Bharatanatyam, Kathak, Odissi, etc.) and their states of origin, classical music (Hindustani & Carnatic), UNESCO World Heritage Sites in India, and major festivals.

topics.name: Government Schemes
topics.notes: Flagship schemes: Ayushman Bharat, PM-Kisan, Jan Dhan Yojana, Swachh Bharat, Ujjwala Yojana, Make in India, Digital India, Skill India â€” know the launch year and core objective of each.

topics.name: Static GK
topics.notes: Important days (Republic Day â€” 26 Jan, National Science Day â€” 28 Feb, etc.), national symbols, authors of famous books, and headquarters of major international organisations (UN, WHO, IMF).

topics.name: Computer Awareness
topics.notes: Basic hardware/software terms, input/output devices, internet basics, common file extensions, generations of computers, and MS Office basics.

topics.name: Indian Railways GK
topics.notes: Indian Railways was founded in 1853 (first train ran Mumbaiâ€“Thane). It's divided into zones & divisions under the Railway Board. Know gauge types (broad/metre/narrow) and recent projects (Vande Bharat, dedicated freight corridors, the bullet train project).
